text.title: Design Patterns Overview Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the patterns-overview methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: A pattern MUST be selected only after a concrete symptom (latency, error rate, merge conflict, change-impact spread) is documented. Patterns chosen without a symptom add indirection cost with no benefit.
rule.rationale: Patterns are answers to specific problems; speculative application produces over-engineered code that future readers struggle to navigate.

rule.statement: Pattern selection MUST include an indirection-cost estimate (extra classes / files / runtime hops) and a problem-pattern-fit score. Reject patterns whose cost outweighs the documented symptom severity.
rule.rationale: Decorator stacking, deep Strategy trees, and over-eager Builders cost readability; cost-aware selection prevents pattern-spam.

rule.statement: Cloud-native patterns (Circuit Breaker, Bulkhead, Saga, CQRS, Event Sourcing) MUST be justified by recorded distributed-systems failures or evidence-backed scaling needs, not by 'we will need it later'.

rationale.source: Microservices.io patterns catalog; cloud-native failure case studies.

rule.rationale: These patterns carry significant operational tax (state machines, projections, idempotency); they pay back only against real distributed failures.

rule.statement: ADR MUST list at least two rejected patterns with reasons. A single-pattern ADR signals the catalog was not consulted.
rule.rationale: Naming rejected alternatives forces breadth-first thinking and produces evidence for future review.

rule.statement: ADR MUST include a review trigger naming an observable signal that would invalidate the chosen pattern (e.g. 'Circuit Breaker tripped < N times / 30 days → consider removal').
rule.rationale: Patterns that stay after their problem is gone become dead weight; the review trigger is the safety net.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (problem statement, evidence, code/design draft, catalog reference), apply r1 → r2 → r3 → r4 → r5 and emit the ADR.
rule.rationale: Order encodes risk: symptom first, cost second, catalog match third, alternatives fourth, review last.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) speculative application, (b) trivial CRUD, (c) pattern already mandated. Note constraint and exit.
rule.rationale: Ceremony in these contexts adds nothing.
